skill.name: fastapi-router-py
skill.description: Create FastAPI routers with CRUD operations, authentication dependencies, and proper response models. Use when building REST API endpoints, creating new routes, implementing CRUD operations, or adding authenticated endpoints in FastAPI applications.
skill.summary: Create consistent FastAPI router patterns for CRUD endpoints, authentication dependencies, response models, and status codes.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks as Ruby shell execution and ordinary FastAPI guidance as weak cryptography. Manual review found only documentation and Python examples in SKILL.md, with no executable commands, prompt injection, network calls, secret access, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: The external command findings are Markdown inline code and fenced Python examples. They document placeholder names, FastAPI dependency patterns, route decorators, and integration paths; they do not execute shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged syntax appears inside Markdown documentation or Python code fences. No command interpreter, Ruby code, subprocess call, or shell invocation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings are documentation text about FastAPI routers, authentication dependencies, and HTTP status codes. No hash function, cipher, key handling, or cryptographic API appears at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain plain documentation headings and descriptions. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic implementation.

content.value_statement: FastAPI teams need consistent router structure across CRUD endpoints and authenticated routes. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code concise patterns for response models, dependencies, status codes, and integration steps.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation of FastAPI routers for REST API endpoints.
Shows optional and required authentication dependency patterns.
Demonstrates response model usage for single-item and list routes.
Documents status codes for create and delete operations.
Lists integration steps for routers, models, services, and frontend API functions.
limitations: Does not include the referenced template file in the current package.
Does not provide complete service layer or database implementation details.
Does not define project-specific authentication functions.
Requires users to adapt names, models, and paths to their application.

use_cases.title: Create a New Resource Router
use_cases.description: Generate consistent route structure for a new FastAPI resource with CRUD endpoint conventions.

use_cases.title: Add Authenticated Endpoints
use_cases.description: Apply required or optional user dependencies when adding protected REST endpoints.

use_cases.title: Standardize API Patterns
use_cases.description: Align route decorators, response models, and status codes across a FastAPI codebase.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Router
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple resource router.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a FastAPI router for a Project resource using this skill. Include list, get, create, update, and delete endpoints.

prompt_templates.title: Add Authentication
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a FastAPI router for Team resources. Use required authentication for write operations and optional authentication for read operations.

prompt_templates.title: Review Router Consistency
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of existing route design.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my FastAPI router for consistency with this skill. Check response models, status codes, authentication dependencies, and integration steps.

prompt_templates.title: Design Full API Slice
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request that spans router integration and supporting layers.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a FastAPI API slice for Tasks using this skill. Include router structure, Pydantic model needs, service layer boundaries, and frontend API function requirements.

output_examples.output: A structured plan for project routes, including list, detail, create, update, and delete operations with matching response models.

output_examples.input: Add authentication to a team endpoint.
output_examples.output: Guidance on where to use required user dependencies for protected actions and optional dependencies for public reads.
A review that confirms create operations return created status and delete operations return no-content status where appropriate.

best_practices: Define response models for every route before implementing handler logic.
Use required authentication for protected write actions.
Mount each router in the main application after creating it.
anti_patterns: Do not leave routes without response models when the return shape is known.
Do not mix optional and required authentication without a clear access rule.
Do not create routers without matching Pydantic models and integration steps.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create FastAPI routers for REST endpoints with CRUD patterns, authentication dependencies, response models, and status codes.

faq.question: Does it include a working template file?
faq.answer: No. The documentation references a template path, but the current package only contains SKILL.md.

faq.question: Can it define my authentication functions?
faq.answer: No. It shows dependency patterns, but your application must provide the actual authentication functions.

faq.question: Can it generate service layer code?
faq.answer: It can outline service layer needs, but the skill mainly documents router-level patterns.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: It is marked as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Manual review found only documentation and example Python snippets, with no confirmed dangerous behavior.
